title: Mythos and the Unverified Cage: Z3-Based Pre-Deployment Verification for Frontier-Model Sandbox Infrastructure
abstract: The April 2026 Claude Mythos sandbox escape exposed a critical weakness in frontier AI containment: the infrastructure surrounding advanced models remains susceptible to formally characterizable arithmetic vulnerabilities. Anthropic has not publicly characterized the escape vector; some secondary accounts hypothesize a CWE-190 arithmetic vulnerability in sandbox networking code. We treat this as unverified and analyze the vulnerability class rather than the specific escape. This paper presents COBALT, a Z3 SMT-based formal verification engine for identifying CWE-190/191/195 arithmetic vulnerability patterns in C/C++ infrastructure prior to deployment. We distinguish two classes of contribution. Validated: COBALT detects arithmetic vulnerability patterns in production codebases, producing SAT verdicts with concrete witnesses and UNSAT guarantees under explicit safety bounds. We demonstrate this on four production case studies: NASA cFE, wolfSSL, Eclipse Mosquitto, and NASA F Prime, with reproducible encodings, verified solver output, and acknowledged security outcomes. Proposed: a four-layer containment framework consisting of COBALT, VERDICT, DIRECTIVE-4, and SENTINEL, mapping pre-deployment verification, pre-execution constraints, output control, and runtime monitoring to the failure modes exposed by the Mythos incident. Under explicit assumptions, we further argue that the publicly reported Mythos escape class is consistent with a Z3-expressible CWE-190 arithmetic formulation and that pre-deployment formal analysis would have been capable of surfacing the relevant pattern. The broader claim is infrastructural: frontier-model safety cannot depend on behavioral safeguards alone; the containment stack itself must be subjected to formal verification.
title_zh: 神话与未经验证的牢笼：基于Z3的前沿模型沙箱基础设施部署前验证
abstract_zh:
2026年4月发生的Claude Mythos沙箱逃逸事件暴露了前沿人工智能系统在安全隔离方面的一个关键弱点：围绕先进模型的基础设施仍然容易受到形式化可描述的算术漏洞影响。Anthropic公司尚未公开说明此次逃逸的具体路径；一些二手资料推测，这可能源于沙箱网络代码中存在CWE-190类型的算术溢出漏洞。我们视此说法为未经证实，并选择聚焦于该类漏洞本身而非具体事件细节。

本文提出COBALT——一种基于Z3 SMT求解器的形式化验证引擎，用于在部署前识别C/C++基础设施代码中的CWE-190、CWE-191和CWE-195等算术漏洞模式。我们的贡献分为两类：

**已验证的成果**：COBALT能够在实际生产级代码库中检测出算术漏洞模式，生成带有具体反例（concrete witnesses）的SAT判定结果，并在明确的安全边界下提供UNSAT（无漏洞）的形式化保证。我们在四个真实生产系统案例中进行了验证：NASA cFE、wolfSSL、Eclipse Mosquitto 和 NASA F'Prime，提供了可复现的编码实现、经验证的求解器输出以及被相关项目确认的安全修复结果。

**提出的框架**：我们进一步提出一个四层防护体系，包括COBALT、VERDICT、DIRECTIVE-4 和 SENTINEL，分别对应部署前验证、执行前约束、输出控制与运行时监控，针对Mythos事件所暴露的各类失效模式进行系统性防御。在明确假设条件下，我们论证指出：公众报道的Mythos逃逸类型与可通过Z3表达的CWE-190算术漏洞形式一致，且若在部署前进行形式化分析，本有可能发现并阻止此类漏洞。

本文更广泛的主张是结构性的：前沿模型的安全不能仅依赖行为层面的防护机制；其底层隔离架构本身也必须接受形式化验证。唯有如此，才能构建真正可信的AI安全基础设施。

title: Visualising CTL Witnesses and Counterexamples -- Extended Version
abstract: One of the advantages of LTL over CTL is that the notion of a counterexample is easy to grasp, visualise and process: it is a trace that violates the property at hand. In this paper we propose a notion of evidence for CTL properties on explicit-state models -- which equally serves as witness for satisfied properties and counterexample for violated ones -- and how to visualise it, with the main aim of (human) comprehension. The main contribution consists of a formal model of evidence, a characterisation of minimal evidence per temporal operator, and a concrete, implemented proposal for its visualisation. This is the extended version of a paper published in SPIN 2026, containing the proofs of all results.
title_zh: CTL反例与证例的可视化——扩展版本
abstract_zh: LTL 相对于 CTL 的优势之一在于，反例的概念易于理解、可视化和处理：它是一条违反相应性质的执行轨迹。在本文中，我们提出了一种针对显式状态模型上 CTL 性质的“证据”概念——该证据既可作为满足性质时的证明，也可作为性质被违反时的反例，并探讨了其可视化方法，主要目标是提升人类的理解能力。本文的主要贡献包括：一个形式化的证据模型、针对每个时态算子的最小证据的特征刻画，以及一种具体且已实现的可视化方案。本文是发表于 SPIN 2026 的论文的扩展版本，包含了所有结果的完整证明。

title: Automatic Code and Test Generation of Smart Contracts from Coordination Models
authors: Elvis Konjoh Selabi, Maurizio Murgia, António Ravara, Emilio Tuosto
abstract: We propose a formal approach for specifying and implementing decentralised coordination in distributed systems, with a focus on smart contracts. Our model captures dynamic roles, data-driven transitions, and external coordination interfaces, enabling high-level reasoning about decentralised workflows. We implement a toolchain that supports formal model validation, code generation for Solidity (our framework is extendable to other smart contract languages), and automated test synthesis. Although our implementation targets blockchain platforms, the methodology is platform-agnostic and may generalise to other service-oriented and distributed architectures. We demonstrate the expressiveness and practicality of the approach by modelling and realising some coordination patterns in smart contracts.
title_zh: 基于协调模型的智能合约自动生成代码与测试
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于在分布式系统中形式化地描述和实现去中心化协调的方法，重点关注智能合约。我们的模型能够捕捉动态角色、数据驱动的状态转换以及外部协调接口，从而支持对去中心化工作流进行高层级的推理。我们实现了一个工具链，支持形式化模型验证、生成Solidity代码（我们的框架可扩展至其他智能合约语言）以及自动化测试生成。尽管当前实现主要面向区块链平台，但该方法本身与平台无关，可推广至其他面向服务和分布式架构的系统。我们通过建模并实现若干智能合约中的协调模式，展示了该方法的表达能力和实用性。

title: Fresh Masking Makes NTT Pipelines Composable: Machine-Checked Proofs for Arithmetic Masking in PQC Hardware
abstract: Post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) accelerators for ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and ML-DSA (FIPS 204) rely on pipelined Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) stages over $\mathbb{Z}_q$. Our prior work established structural dependency analysis at scale [1] and quantified the security margin of partial NTT masking [2]. Whether per-stage arithmetic masking guarantees pipeline-level security had no prior machine-checked answer for the r-bearing case: composition frameworks (ISW, t-SNI, PINI, DOM) were formalized exclusively for Boolean masking over $\mathrm{GF}(2)$; no proof assistant artifact addresses the NTT butterfly over $\mathbb{Z}_q$. We present three machine-checked results in Lean 4 with Mathlib, all zero sorry. First, we close a stated limitation of prior work: value-independence implies constant marginal distribution under fresh randomness (via an algebraic MutualInfoZero proxy). Second, butterfly per-context uniformity: for any Cooley-Tukey butterfly with fresh output mask over $\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z}$ ($q > 0$), each output wire has exactly one mask value producing each output, a uniform marginal independent of secrets, universal over all moduli, twiddle factors, and inputs. Third, a k-stage NTT pipeline with fresh per-stage masking satisfies per-context uniformity at every stage under the ISW first-order probing model. We document a named warning: pointwise value-independence is false for butterfly outputs. The Adams Bridge accelerator (CHIPS Alliance Caliptra) fails the fresh masking hypothesis, masking active only in INTT round 0, architecturally explaining its structural insecurity. Artifact: nine theorems, 1,738 build jobs, zero sorry. Composition for nonlinear gadgets (Barrett) is addressed in forthcoming manuscripts proving Barrett's PF-PINI(2) satisfaction ('one-bit barrier') [3] and k-stage composition for PF-PINI gadgets under fresh-mask renewal [4].
title_zh: 新颖的掩码机制使NTT流水线具有可组合性：针对PQC硬件中算术掩码的机器验证证明
abstract_zh:
面向ML-KEM（FIPS 203）和ML-DSA（FIPS 204）的后量子密码学（PQC）加速器依赖于在 $\mathbb{Z}_q$ 上进行流水线化的数论变换（NTT）阶段。我们之前的工作建立了大规模的结构依赖性分析 [1]，并量化了部分NTT掩码的安全裕度 [2]。然而，每一阶段的算术掩码是否能保证整个流水线级别的安全性，在涉及r承载情形下此前尚无机器验证的答案：现有的组合安全框架（如ISW、t-SNI、PINI、DOM）仅针对在 $\mathrm{GF}(2)$ 上的布尔掩码形式化；没有任何证明助手工具处理过在 $\mathbb{Z}_q$ 上的NTT蝶形运算。

本文在Lean 4与Mathlib中提供了三个完全机器验证的结果，全部实现“零sorry”（即无未证引理）。第一，我们解决了先前工作提出的一个明确局限：通过代数化的互信息为零（MutualInfoZero）代理，证明了值独立性意味着在引入新鲜随机性时具有恒定的边缘分布。第二，蝶形单元的上下文无关均匀性：对于任意采用新鲜输出掩码的Cooley-Tukey蝶形单元，在 $\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z}$（$q > 0$）上，每个输出线恰好对应一个掩码值以生成每一个可能输出，其边缘分布是均匀的，且独立于秘密信息，适用于所有模数、旋转因子和输入。第三，一个k阶段的NTT流水线若在每阶段使用新鲜掩码，则在ISW一阶探测模型下，每一阶段均满足上下文无关均匀性。

我们记录了一个命名警告：蝶形输出的逐点值独立性不成立。Adams Bridge加速器（CHIPS Alliance Caliptra）未能满足新鲜掩码假设，其掩码机制仅在逆NTT（INTT）第0轮激活，这一架构设计缺陷从结构上解释了其安全隐患。

成果附带材料：九个定理，1,738次构建任务，零未完成证明。关于非线性组件（如Barrett乘法）的组合安全性将在即将发表的论文中讨论，包括证明Barrett满足PF-PINI(2)（突破“单比特障碍”）[3]，以及在掩码更新条件下PF-PINI组件的k阶段组合安全性 [4]。

title: NASchecker: Automatically Identifying the Performance, Security, and Privacy Issues of NAS Devices
authors: Guangyue Ren, Jincheng Wang, Le Yu, Liping Han, Mingzhe Hu, Wei Chen, Tingting Liu, Xiapu Luo, Guozi Sun
abstract: Network attached storage (NAS) devices are widely deployed for personal data storage. However, their distributed architecture and limited inspection interfaces pose significant challenges for comprehensive performance, security, and privacy analysis. In this paper, we first establish a threat model for NAS ecosystems. Then, we present a systematic framework NASchecker for discovering performance optimization mechanisms, security threats, and privacy leakage in NAS devices. By analyzing traffic generated during varied file operations on crafted files, NASchecker infers implemented optimizations and identifies security flaws within the traffic (e.g., susceptibility to passive sniffing and replay attacks). NASchecker also integrates NAS-specific protocol fuzzing and firmware reverse engineering to uncover deep-seated command injection, memory corruption, and improper access control vulnerabilities. NASchecker compares personally identifiable information (PII) leaked in traffic against declarations in privacy policies to detect privacy compliance issues. We evaluated NASchecker on twelve commercial NAS devices. Our results reveal that none of the tested devices employ file compression or deduplication. From a security standpoint, ten devices are vulnerable to passive sniffing and seven to replay attacks. Moreover, seven devices are affected by command injection, four by memory corruption, and eleven by improper access control. From a privacy perspective, four devices leaked PIIs that were not disclosed in their respective privacy policies. After reporting the findings to the manufacturers, we have been acknowledged by several manufacturers, resulting in the assignment of 20 CVEs and 6 NVDB entries (16 of them are rated as high severity). These findings validate NASchecker’s effectiveness and underscore the urgent need for improved design and testing practices of NAS.
title_zh: NASchecker：自动识别NAS设备的性能、安全与隐私问题
abstract_zh: 网络附加存储（NAS）设备被广泛用于个人数据存储。然而，由于其分布式架构以及有限的检测接口，对这些设备进行全面的性能、安全性和隐私性分析面临巨大挑战。本文中，我们首先建立了针对NAS生态系统威胁模型。然后，提出了一种系统性框架NASchecker，用于发现NAS设备中的性能优化机制、安全威胁和隐私泄露问题。通过分析在精心构造的文件上执行各种文件操作时产生的网络流量，NASchecker能够推断出已实现的优化措施，并识别流量中存在的安全漏洞（例如易受被动嗅探和重放攻击的影响）。NASchecker还结合了针对NAS特定协议的模糊测试和固件逆向工程技术，以揭示深层次的命令注入、内存损坏和访问控制不当等漏洞。此外，NASchecker将流量中泄露的个人身份信息（PII）与其隐私政策声明进行比对，从而检测隐私合规性问题。我们在十二款商用NAS设备上评估了NASchecker的有效性。结果表明，所有被测设备均未采用文件压缩或去重技术；从安全性角度看，十款设备易受被动嗅探攻击，七款易受重放攻击；此外，七款设备存在命令注入漏洞，四款存在内存损坏问题，十一款存在访问控制不当问题。从隐私角度来看，四款设备泄露了其隐私政策中未声明的PII信息。在我们将相关发现报告给厂商后，已获得多家厂商的认可，并因此获得了20个CVE编号和6个NVDB条目（其中16个被评定为高危级别）。这些结果验证了NASchecker的有效性，也凸显了改进NAS设备设计与测试实践的迫切需求。

title: 2025 IEEE 34th Asian Test Symposium (ATS)
title_zh: 2025 年 IEEE 第 34 届亚洲测试研讨会（ATS）

title:
Deterministic Dual-Role I
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    C Verification via Bus Ownership Invariants and Bounded Runtime Switching
authors: Wei-Tzer Huang, Yu-Cheng Liao, Wei-Chen Lin
abstract: Dual-role I2C endpoints have become a practical necessity in modern system-on-chip (SoC) architectures, where a single IP must alternate between master and slave roles across boot, recovery, and power-management phases. However, existing verification environments are predominantly designed under static role assumptions, and attempts to retrofit runtime switching often lead to ambiguous bus ownership, unsafe mid-transaction transitions, and concurrency-induced deadlocks. These challenges stem from the absence of explicit safety and progress contracts governing dynamic bus authority. This work presents a deterministic dual-role Universal Verification Methodology(UVM) architecture that formalizes bus driving authority as an explicitly managed resource. The proposed framework defines Bus Ownership Invariants (BOIs) at the open-drain drive-enable interface, enforcing electrical safety directly at the resolution surface. Role transitions are restricted to protocol-aligned commit boundaries—monitor-observed STOP followed by the bus-free interval (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t_{\mathrm {BUF}}$ </tex-math></inline-formula>)—ensuring release-before-commit discipline. A bounded event-driven synchronization mechanism further decouples transaction intent from pin-level authority, eliminating cyclic dependencies and guaranteeing deadlock-free progress. Experimental validation using SystemVerilog/UVM and co-simulated I2C RTL models across 165 regression runs demonstrates deterministic mixed-role execution, strict preservation of clock authority in slave mode, and bounded switching latency (median <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.3~\mu s$ </tex-math></inline-formula>, maximum <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.2~\mu s$ </tex-math></inline-formula>) without long-tail stalls. The resulting methodology provides reproducible safety and liveness guarantees for runtime dual-role verification.
title_zh: 通过总线所有权不变式和有界运行时切换实现确定性双角色 I²C 验证
abstract_zh:
双角色I2C端点已成为现代片上系统（SoC）架构中的实际必需，其中单一IP核需在启动、恢复及电源管理等不同阶段间切换主/从角色。然而，现有的验证环境大多基于静态角色假设设计，尝试引入运行时角色切换常导致总线所有权模糊、事务中途切换不安全以及并发引发的死锁等问题。这些挑战源于缺乏对动态总线控制权进行明确定义的安全性与进展保障机制。

本文提出一种确定性的双角色通用验证方法学（UVM）架构，将总线驱动权限建模为显式管理的资源。所提出的框架在开漏型驱动使能接口层面定义了“总线所有权不变量”（Bus Ownership Invariants, BOIs），直接在信号解析层面上保证电气安全性。角色切换被严格限制在协议对齐的提交边界执行——即监测到STOP条件后，必须经过总线空闲间隔（<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t_{\mathrm{BUF}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>）——从而确保“先释放、再获取”的操作纪律。此外，通过一种有界事件驱动的同步机制，进一步解耦事务意图与引脚级控制权，消除循环依赖，保障无死锁的进展性。

基于SystemVerilog/UVM实现，并结合I2C RTL模型进行协同仿真，在165次回归测试中开展实验验证。结果表明：该方法可实现确定性的混合角色执行，从机模式下时钟控制权得到严格保护，且角色切换延迟有界（中位数<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.3~\mu s$</tex-math></inline-formula>，最大值<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.2~\mu s$</tex-math></inline-formula>），未出现长尾阻塞现象。该方法为运行时双角色功能的验证提供了可重现的安全性与活性保障。
